name: OpenAI o3
archDisplay: Dense transformer reasoning model (exact size undisclosed)
note: Flagship reasoning model with native tool use; major gains on math, coding, and ARC-AGI. Price reflects OpenAI's June 2025 80% cut from the original $10/$40 launch price.

results.notes: MathVista visual math reasoning, o3, high reasoning effort. Read directly from a mirrored copy of OpenAI's official launch blog (o1=71.8, o3=86.8, o4-mini=72.0) and cross-confirmed via independent web search corroboration.

CharXiv-Reasoning (scientific figure reasoning drawn from arXiv papers), o3, high reasoning effort; from OpenAI's official launch blog (o1=55.1, o3=78.6, o4-mini=72.0). The blog notes this and MathVista were 'updated April 16 to reflect a system prompt change that wasn't present in the original evaluation.'

BrowseComp agentic browsing, o3 with python+browsing tools, high reasoning effort. From OpenAI's official launch blog chart (4o+browsing=1.9, o3=49.7, o4-mini=28.3, deep research=51.5); confirmed by independent secondary-source search corroboration.

Aider Polyglot code editing, diff format, o3-high (whole-format score is 81.3%). Diff-format figure used to match this corpus's existing aider-polyglot convention (e.g. gpt-5.json uses the diff figure). [score corrected during round-5 full-corpus accuracy re-verify: 81.3 -> 79.6]

Princeton HAL 'HAL Generalist Agent' scaffold, o3 at 'Medium' reasoning effort (April 2025 snapshot) -- only effort level tested for o3 on this leaderboard, so no variant ambiguity. GAIA validation set.

ARC-AGI-1 Semi-Private Eval V1, o3-medium, $2.52/task — official production/released o3 (April 2025), tested by ARC Prize after confirming this differs from the December 2024 o3-preview. o3-low scored 41%; o3-high was excluded from the leaderboard due to insufficient task coverage (only 37/100 tasks returned).

ARC-AGI-2 Semi-Private Eval, o3-medium (released April 2025 model, not the Dec-2024 preview). ARC Prize's official leaderboard currently rounds this to 3.0%. Neither o3-low nor o3-medium exceeded 3% on ARC-AGI-2, versus a 60% average-human baseline.

FrontierMath, o3 with high reasoning effort, independently measured by Epoch AI at 10% (±2%) — notably lower than OpenAI's original ~25% claim from the December 2024 pre-release demo, attributed to using the actual released model, an updated/expanded problem set (290 problems vs. the original 180), and no special internal scaffold/compute. Reported alongside o4-mini (17%) in the same evaluation.

verificationNotes: Confirmed release 2025-04-16 via OpenAI/TechCrunch. Pricing $2/$8 verified as OpenAI's current price after an 80% cut on 2025-06-10 from the original $10/$40 launch price (confirmed via OpenAI Devs official X post and VentureBeat) — note added to clarify this isn't the launch price. Direct fetch of openai.com/index/introducing-o3-and-o4-mini/ returned 403, but all 6 benchmark rows (AIME 91.6, GPQA 83.3, SWE-bench 69.1, MMMU 82.9, HLE 24.9-with-tools, Codeforces 2706) were independently corroborated verbatim by multiple secondary sources quoting OpenAI's official launch figures — distinct from the earlier Dec-2024 o3 preview-announcement numbers (e.g. 96.7% AIME), confirming these are the correct April-2025 released-model figures.
